mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 6 months signals tightening financial conditions that raise the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing a negative drift in S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days regardless of level.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trailing 6-month slope and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive FEDFUNDS trends are followed by positive returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points within a single month from a cyclical low, it signals an inflection in labor market health that triggers rapid equity de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.2pp month-over-month from a 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% in March 2006, near multi-year lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the effect disappears when excluding NBER recession windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in HOUST alongside a still-elevated or rising FEDFUNDS level indicates that monetary tightening is actively suppressing housing demand, a leading indicator of broader consumption and credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. Neither series alone captures this because rate levels condition how strongly housing weakness transmits to equities.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage near 2120K units in Feb 2006, off 2005 peak
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing market cooling
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-decline/high-FEDFUNDS trigger produces forward 60-day returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or single-series HOUST decline alone explains equivalent predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, continued acceleration in INDPRO signals capacity utilization pressures that presage margin compression and future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days, whereas the same acceleration in early-cycle recovery would be bullish.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value 111.2088 in March 2006 reflecting continued industrial expansion
failure_condition: During periods with INDPRO YoY growth > 3% late in an expansion, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances, contradicting the hypothesized negative drift.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS approaches a plateau after a prolonged tightening cycle (successive meeting-to-meeting increases slow to near zero), markets anticipate the end of rate hikes and re-rate equities upward over the subsequent quarter, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month change falls below 0.10pp after at least 12 consecutive months of increases
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.59% in March 2006 after prolonged 2004-2006 tightening cycle
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns following plateau triggers are negative or flat (mean <= 0) in more than 45% of historical instances, or effect is not distinguishable from unconditional 60-day return distribution.

mechanism_narrative: Continued positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects sustained labor income generation supporting consumption and corporate revenue expectations, producing a small persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage_value 134966K in March 2006 showing continued employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02 across full sample, or hit rate for positive PAYEMS months predicting positive returns is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth, approximated by RSAFS growth outpacing CPIAUCSL inflation, indicates genuine consumer demand strength beyond price effects, and this combined signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; nominal RSAFS growth alone conflates inflation with real demand and is a weaker signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth exceeds CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation by more than 2pp
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 361046M in March 2006, CPIAUCSL at 199.1 in Feb 2006
failure_condition: The real-retail-sales-growth trigger fails to produce forward 20-day returns exceeding the unconditional mean by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime with GDPC1 growth decelerating relative to its trailing trend while INDPRO remains elevated, the divergence signals an unsustainable production overhang that precedes broad economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; in early-cycle regimes this same divergence is not predictive.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth decelerates for 2 consecutive quarters while INDPRO year-over-year growth remains above 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised_value 16136.734B reflecting continued but potentially decelerating expansion into 2006
failure_condition: The GDPC1-deceleration/INDPRO-elevated joint trigger yields forward 60-day returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or is only significant when tested in early-cycle regimes, invalidating regime specificity.
